{"description":"Explore project structure","prompt":"Explore the project at /home/user/workspace thoroughly. I need to understand:
1. The overall project structure (list all files and directories)
2. The `pages/` directory contents and all files within it
3. The `package.json` (Next.js version, dependencies)
4. The `tsconfig.json` if it exists
5. Any existing `app/` directory
6. Any shared components, lib, utils, hooks directories
7. The `next.config.js` or `next.config.mjs`
8. The `public/` directory
9. Any middleware files
10. Any layout files or custom App files (\_app.tsx, \_document.tsx)

Be very thorough - read every file in the pages/ directory and any related lib/components/api directories. I need to see the FULL content of every file.","subagent_type":"Explore"}
attributes.io.output:
[{"type":"text","text":"I have now read every single file in the project. Here is the complete report.

---

## Full Project Exploration Report

### 1. Overall Project Structure (All Files)

The project is located at `/home/user/workspace` and has 13 source files across 4 non-git, non-node_modules directories:

```
/home/user/workspace/
  .gitignore
  next.config.ts
  package.json
  tsconfig.json
  package-lock.json
  components/
    AppProvider.js
  pages/
    _app.js
    _document.js
    _error.js
    404.js
    index.js
    api/
      posts/
        index.js
        [id].js
    blog/
      index.js
      [id].js
  styles/
    globals.css
```

No `app/` directory exists. No `lib/`, `utils/`, or `hooks/` directories exist. No `public/` directory exists. No middleware file exists.

---

### 2. Pages Directory -- Full Contents

**`/home/user/workspace/pages/index.js`** -- The homepage. Uses `getServerSideProps` to SSR-fetch 5 posts from JSONPlaceholder on each request. Passes `posts`, `userAgent`, and a `timestamp` to the component. Renders a welcome heading, the server timestamp, the user agent string, a list of recent post links, and a "View All Posts" button that uses `router.push('/blog')`.

**`/home/user/workspace/pages/_app.js`** -- Custom App component. Wraps every page in `<AppProvider>` (from `components/AppProvider`), renders a global header with nav links ("Home" and "Blog"), a `<main>` with the page component, and a footer. Imports `globals.css`.

**`/home/user/workspace/pages/_document.js`** -- Custom Document. Sets `<Html lang="en">`, includes meta description, favicon, a Google Fonts preconnect/preload for Inter (weights 400/500/600). Renders `<Main />` and `<NextScript />`.

**`/home/user/workspace/pages/_error.js`** -- Custom Error page. Displays the status code (different messages for server vs client errors). Uses `getInitialProps` to extract the status code from the response or error. Handles 404 with a "This page could not be found" message.

**`/home/user/workspace/pages/404.js`** -- Custom 404 page. Displays "404 - Page Not Found" with a "Go Back Home" button using `router.push('/')`.

**`/home/user/workspace/pages/blog/index.js`** -- Blog list page. Uses `getStaticProps` with ISR (`revalidate: 60`) to fetch all posts from JSONPlaceholder. Renders a grid of post cards, each showing the title, a 100-char excerpt, and a "Read More" button using `router.push`.

**`/home/user/workspace/pages/blog/[id].js`** -- Individual blog post page. Uses `getStaticPaths` with `fallback: 'blocking'` to pre-render the first 10 posts. In `getStaticProps`, fetches both the post and its comments from JSONPlaceholder in parallel. ISR with `revalidate: 300`. The component renders a back button (`router.back()`), the post title/body, and a comments section. Handles the `isFallback` state with a "Loading..." div. Falls back to `notFound: true` on fetch error.

**`/home/user/workspace/pages/api/posts/index.js`** -- API route for `/api/posts`. Handles:
- `GET` -- returns a hardcoded array of 2 posts (id, title, content).
- `POST` -- validates `title` and `content` in the body, returns 400 if missing; otherwise simulates creating a post with `Date.now()` as the ID.
- Any other method -- returns 405 with Allow header `['GET', 'POST']`.

**`/home/user/workspace/pages/api/posts/[id].js`** -- API route for `/api/posts/[id]`. Handles:
- `GET` -- returns a simulated post object using the `id` from the query.
- `PUT` -- simulates updating a post (title/content from body, or defaults).
- `DELETE` -- returns a success message `"Post {id} deleted successfully"`.
- Any other method -- returns 405 with Allow header `['GET', 'PUT', 'DELETE']`.

---

### 3. package.json (Next.js version, dependencies)

**File:** `/home/user/workspace/package.json`

- **Next.js version:** `16.2.0-canary.41` (a canary/pre-release build of Next.js 16)
- **React version:** `1…(+4902)
